description_long: This is a vintage steam locomotive with a weathered red and black exterior, featuring a cylindrical boiler, a large smokestack, and a prominent headlamp at the front. The locomotive has a classic cab at the rear, covered by a black roof, and is mounted on large spoked wheels with visible connecting rods. Brass and metallic details, such as a bell and dome, are present on the top of the boiler, adding to its historical character. The bodywork shows signs of rust and wear, suggesting long-term use or exposure to the elements. The side panels display a nameplate and a number plate, typical of traditional steam engines. There are no visible passenger or cargo carriages attached, and the train appears to be a standalone engine.
description: A weathered red and black vintage steam locomotive with large spoked wheels, a covered cab, and brass detailing. The engine shows signs of rust and age, with no visible carriages attached.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular headlamp, smokestack, and the front buffer beam with couplers.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the full length of the locomotive, large wheels, side rods, and the cab with visible nameplate.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cab, the coal bunker area, and the rear buffer beam.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the wheels, rods, and cab details, with the number plate visible.
attribute_door_placement: doors at each end of the cab
attribute_window_arrangement: spaced rectangular windows on the cab
